summaries.headline: Christopher and Rosaria Pounce Early
summaries.text: A tidy opening day for Christopher and Rosaria. Christopher’s South Korea nicked a 2-1 win over Carolina’s Czech Republic, while Rosaria doubled up as Mexico beat Rosaria’s South Africa 2-0. So Christopher leads on goal difference, with Rosaria right behind on points. Carolina is the early straggler after that narrow defeat, and South Africa are off to a rum start too. Best overall: Christopher. Worst overall: Carolina. Plenty of time left, of course, but the table’s already got a bit of a swagger about it.

summaries.headline: Vicki Storms Clear, Max Left in the Mire
summaries.text: Vicki’s USA thumped Max’s Paraguay 4-1, the day’s big swing and a proper boost to the top of the table. Meanwhile Henry’s Canada and Chris’s Bosnia & Herzegovina played out a tidy 1-1, so neither’s running away with it just yet. Best overall is still Vicki on 3 points and a cracking +3 goal difference. Christopher and Rosaria are also on 3, but Vicki’s got the better numbers. At the other end, Max is having a grim time at the foot of the pile on 0 points and -3. Plenty of tournament left, but that’s a nasty early wobble.

summaries.headline: Nana Lands a Peach, Henry Hang On
summaries.text: Australia did the business for Nana, while Henry’s Scotland and Qatar both pinched points, keeping him top of the lot on 5. Jack had a mixed day: Scotland beat his Haiti, but Switzerland’s draw at least stopped the rot. Chris’s Turkey fell 2-0 to Nana, and Max could only share the spoils with Morocco. Best overall is Henry, still leading the sweep; worst is the 0-point gang, with Alex, Emily, Grandad and Sofia yet to get off the mark. A proper scrap at the top, that.

summaries.headline: Christopher’s Chasing the Chips
summaries.text: Big day for Christopher: Germany’s 7-1 rout over Curaçao was a proper demolition job, while Emily also had a belter with Sweden thumping Tunisia 5-1. Grandad edged Carolina 1-0, so he’s nicked a useful point too. At the other end, Sara took a hiding with Tunisia and Carolina’s still rooted to the bottom after that narrow loss. Overall, Christopher’s top of the pile on 6 points, with Henry just behind on 5. Emily’s next in with 4, looking lively. Spare a thought for Carolina: no points, grim goal difference, and the wooden spoon is waving at her already.

summaries.headline: Draws on the board, chaos in the pub
summaries.text: A proper day of stalemates: Iran and New Zealand shared four goals, while Saudi Arabia v Uruguay, Belgium v Egypt, and Spain v Cape Verde all finished level. That’s decent enough for Emily and Alex, plus Max, Sara, Sofia, Vicki and Grandad to keep things ticking over. Top of the lot is still Christopher on 6, though Emily and Vicki are breathing down his neck on 5. At the other end, Carolina remains marooned on 0, with Jack, Chris and Sofia stuck on 1. Not much drama in the results, but the table’s still got a few dodgy exits at the bottom.

summaries.headline: Grandad Gathers Pace as Sofia’s Day Goes Awry
summaries.text: A tidy day for Grandad, with Austria and Spain both doing the business, and Nana’s Argentina also swatting aside Algeria. Max enjoyed a proper belter as Norway thumped Iraq 4-1, while Carolina picked up a useful France win. At the other end, Sofia had a stinker: Jordan, Iraq and Senegal all lost, with only Egypt left to rescue pride. Overall, Grandad stays top on 7 points, with Nana and Christopher pressing close behind on 6. Sofia remains bottom on 1 point, still stuck in the mud and looking for a miracle or a very large favour.

summaries.headline: England Fly, Panama Pack Their Bags
summaries.text: A good night for Alex as England battered Croatia 4-2, while Sara nicked a 1-0 win for Ghana over Panama. Chris also kept Colombia ticking with a 3-1 success against Nana’s Uzbekistan, and Jack shared the spoils in a 1-1 with DR Congo. Top of the pile is still Grandad on 7, with Nana and Christopher next on 6. At the wrong end, Sofia remains stuck on 1, while Rosaria, Jack and Carolina are all still trying to find a decent run of form.

summaries.headline: England Thrash It, Nana Takes a Knock
summaries.text: Big day for Alex, whose England belted Croatia 4-2 and gave his sweepstake a proper lift. Sara also nicked a vital 1-0 with Ghana, while Chris took a hit as Colombia beat Nana’s Uzbekistan 3-1. Up top, Grandad still leads on 7, but Nana is right on his heels with 6, and Chris isn’t far back. At the other end, Sofia is still rooted to the floor on 1, with Rosaria and Jack stuck in the chasing pack. Best overall? Grandad. Worst? Sofia, with the table doing her no favours at all.
